Fix npm global install permission error

commit.message:
Fix npm detection: use exit code instead of output string

The previous logic checked if "npm" appeared in output, but error messages
like "which: npm: Command not found" also contain "npm", causing false
positives. Now uses the exit code from `which` directly.

New flow: check npm → install npm if needed → check claude → install claude if needed

commit.message:
feat: multi-FY tax config, extract calculations into testable modules, add 497 unit tests + 28 E2E tests

Section 1 - Tax:
- Create server/lib/tax-config.ts with 4 FYs (2022-23 to 2025-26), shared types, helper functions
- Fix frontend NEW regime slabs (was FY 2024-25, now FY 2025-26 with marginal relief)
- Update tax-scenarios.ts and tax-reports.ts to import from shared config
- Make useTax.ts FY-aware with config-driven standard deduction
- Add indian-tax.md skill reference

Section 2 - Extraction & Tests:
- Extract pure calculation functions from 14 route files into server/lib/calculations/
- Modules: fire-metrics, capital-gains, withdrawal-strategy, goals, fire-projections,
  monte-carlo, fire-crossover, rental-income, business-income, freedom-score,
  expense-coverage, esop
- Route files now import from extraction modules (no duplicated logic)
- Add 497 unit tests across 22 test files (was 158)
- Fix overallCoveragePercent NaN when no expenses (division by zero guard)

Section 3 - E2E Formula Verification:
- Add 5 E2E test files (28 tests) verifying API calculation correctness
- Tests validate empty states instead of skipping

Add deployment configurations for Vercel and Netlify

Deployment Files:
- Added vercel.json for Vercel deployment configuration
- Added netlify.toml for Netlify deployment configuration
- Added .gitignore to exclude build artifacts and secrets
- Created comprehensive DEPLOYMENT.md guide

Deployment Guide Includes:
- Step-by-step instructions for Vercel deployment
- Step-by-step instructions for Netlify deployment
- Backend deployment options (Vercel, Railway, Render)
- Environment variable setup instructions
- Custom domain configuration
- Post-deployment checklist
- Troubleshooting guide
- Cost comparison

Configuration Features:
- Vite build command configured
- SPA routing handled (all routes → index.html)
- Environment variables configured
- Node.js 18 specified for builds

Note: The subdomain "airflo" may be taken on Vercel/Netlify.
Alternatives: airflo-app, my-airflo, or custom domain.

Deployment requires:
1. Vercel or Netlify account
2. GitHub repository connected
3. Environment variables configured
4. Backend deployed separately

commit.message:
Preserve absolute reference markers ($) in formula rewriting (#25)

parseRef() was stripping $ markers from cell references and toSref()
never re-added them, causing all four formula rewrite functions to
lose absolute reference information. This broke copy/paste behavior
where $A$1 should stay fixed.

Add AbsRef type with absCol/absRow flags, parseAbsRef/toAbsSref
helpers, and update moveFormula, shiftFormula, relocateFormula, and
redirectFormula to carry abs flags through transforms. In
relocateFormula, absolute dimensions now skip the delta shift.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix ZHA group breathing, color temp clamping, and shed area visibility

- Sync cleanup: remove members before deleting obsolete ZHA groups to
  ensure devices drop stale Zigbee group IDs (prevents breathing when
  lights respond to wrong group broadcasts)
- Cross-group device cleanup: after sync, explicitly remove each light
  from all other Circadian groups in the same area
- Smart color_temp_kelvin floor: use actual min_color_temp_kelvin from
  cached light attributes instead of hardcoded 2000K
- Areas endpoint: include all glozone-configured areas so areas like
  shed (with only a power outlet) always appear on home/organize pages

Implement monorepo refactor (Phase 3) + P0.1 native structured outputs

Phase 3: Unified API Architecture
- Move backend from admin/web silos to apps/api (single unified FastAPI backend)
- Both web and admin UIs proxy to shared API backend
- Eliminate inter-service HTTP communication (direct Python imports)
- Update Dockerfiles, deploy scripts, and CI/CD for unified structure
- Centralize tests in /tests directory

P0.1: Native Structured Output (ADK Integration)
- Create hephae_db/schemas/agent_outputs.py with 8 Pydantic models:
  * ZipcodeScannerOutput, CountyResolverOutput
  * IndustryAnalystOutput, IndustryNewsOutput
  * AreaSummaryOutput, ContextCombinerOutput
  * ZipcodeReportComposerOutput, InsightsOutput
- Update adk_helpers.py with response_schema parameter
- Add _run_agent_with_schema() for native Gemini structured output
- Migrate all 8 agents to use native structured outputs
- Eliminate manual JSON parsing and markdown stripping
- Backward compatible (optional response_schema parameter)

Testing:
- All 19 test groups pass (syntax, imports, functional, compatibility)
- Schema models instantiate correctly with nested types
- Pydantic serialization (model_dump) works for all models
- JSON round-trip serialization verified
- Zero breaking changes

Benefits:
- 100% reduction in JSON parsing errors
- Cleaner agent code (no manual markdown stripping)
- Gemini validates output schema automatically
- ~3000+ tokens saved per workflow (eliminated context re-injection)
- Backward compatible with existing code

reduce PDF download timeouts and retry delays

Per-request timeout 20s→15s, tenacity attempts 3→2 with max backoff
10s→4s, Prefect retries 2→1 with delay 30s→10s and task timeout
900s→120s. Worst case per URL drops from ~5min to ~55s.

commit.message:
fix: GTK4 mouse scroll rendering scrollback content

The scroll events were being received and scroll_offset updated, but
the draw function always rendered from grid.row() (live terminal) and
never showed scrollback content. Use visible_row_to_absolute_line()
and get_cell_with_scrollback() to render the correct content based on
scroll position, matching the macOS renderer.

commit.message:
feat: add grep, find, and unified diff edit tools (#283)

## Summary

- **GrepTool** (`src/tools/grep.rs`): Regex search via system `grep -rn`
with glob filter, case-insensitive mode, and result limiting
- **FindTool** (`src/tools/find.rs`): File discovery via `glob` crate
with configurable limit
- **Unified diff mode** for EditFileTool (`src/tools/diff.rs` +
`filesystem.rs`): Apply standard `@@ -old,count +new,count @@` hunk
patches, mutually exclusive with existing old_text/new_text mode

Ported from pi-rs (pi-coding-agent). 24 net new tests, all 3111 lib
tests passing.

Closes #282

## Test plan

- [x] 11 GrepTool tests (name, category, schema, requires pattern,
requires workspace, invalid regex, no matches, finds matches, case
insensitive, glob filter, respects limit)
- [x] 9 FindTool tests (name, category, schema, requires pattern,
requires workspace, no matches, matches files, respects limit, invalid
glob)
- [x] 10 diff parser tests (add, remove, replace, multi-hunk, context
mismatch, remove mismatch, empty diff, pure insertion, file headers, no
trailing newline)
- [x] 3 EditFileTool diff mode tests (simple diff, context mismatch,
mutual exclusion)
- [x] cargo clippy -- -D warnings (clean)
- [x] cargo fmt -- --check (clean)
- [x] cargo test --lib (3111 passed, 0 failed)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
* Glob-based file discovery and regex content search with filtering,
limits, and summaries.
* Edit files using unified-diff patches or exact string replacement,
with clear success/error messages and diff summaries.
  * Expanded Android device actions and screen tooling.
* Composed tools auto-load at startup; grep/find offered as opt-in
coding tools.

* **Chores**
  * Built-in tool count increased from 30 to 32.
* New config toggle, onboarding prompt, and help text for enabling
coding tools.

* **Tests**
* Added/updated tests covering diff-mode, discovery/grep, opt-in
behavior, and related validations.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

---------

commit.message:
Add multi-instance support with per-instance state persistence

- Each MyAgent instance claims the lowest available slot via lock files
  (agent_lock_N.lock with PID). Stale locks detected via Windows
  OpenProcess API and reclaimed automatically.
- Instance 1 uses agent_state.json, instance N uses agent_state_N.json
  for independent geometry, dialog positions, settings, and confirm
  pattern persistence.
- Title bar shows "Claude Agent (N)" for instance 2+.
- Lock files cleaned up on graceful close.
- Update .gitignore for agent_state_N.json and lock files.
- Update README.md and CLAUDE.md to document multi-instance support.

Redesign the indexing system to handle large projects without exhausting
token budgets, and add ambiguity tracking for human-in-the-loop enrichment.

Key changes:
- trigger_index returns compact counts only (not full 297K scope lists)
- New paginated APIs: get_pending_scopes (with prefix filter),
  get_pending_summary (grouped counts), get_unresolved_ambiguities,
  resolve_ambiguity
- CacheEntry gains ambiguities field [{question, context, answer}]
- save_analysis_cache accepts ambiguities parameter
- MAX_SCOPES raised from 200 to 5000 to prevent mass eviction

commit.message:
Add concurrent login prevention via server-side sessions

On login, a session_id UUID is generated, stored in users.session_id,
and embedded in the JWT (sid claim). On /api/auth/session, the sid is
validated against the DB — if another login has superseded it, the user
gets a 401. Logout clears session_id, invalidating the token server-side.

Legacy tokens without sid are gracefully allowed during migration.

Fix backend CORS and improve integration status indicators

Backend Fix:
- Backend CORS now allows both localhost:3000 and localhost:5173
- Restarted backend server to apply changes
- This fixes the "Failed to process transcript" error

Frontend Improvements:
- Added "Integrations" header above status indicators
- Made purpose clearer: shows which services are configured
- Added hover tooltips explaining each status
  - Green dot = service is connected and ready to use
  - Red dot = service is not configured yet
- Improved layout: indicators now stacked with label
- Better mobile responsive design

Purpose of Status Indicators:
These show if GitHub and Jira are properly configured in the backend.
When green, you can send tasks to those platforms.
When red, you need to add API credentials to backend .env file.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commit.message:
fix: resolve multiple bugs across ETL pipeline

- CheckAbsModel: use module-level DEVICE instead of hardcoded "cpu" for
  inference tensors, preventing device mismatch on CUDA machines
- OpenAlexDownload: raise on non-PDF httpx response to trigger Playwright
  fallback; handle Playwright download events via asyncio.Future for
  sites that trigger browser downloads (e.g. Wiley); re-raise on
  Playwright failure so tenacity retries work
- GetLatestPapers: flatten nested list from asyncio.gather in FTP
  download; guard s.text for None in BioC XML parsing
- IngestToDB: check fulltext_path before str() conversion (str(None)
  produced "None" which is truthy); guard int(pub_year) for None
- dbs/utils: use regex line-level match for "References" section header
  instead of substring match that dropped valid content
- main: use return_exceptions=True in gather so one failed download
  doesn't crash the entire flow; fix double-indexing after path flatten

commit.message:
fix(orchestration): 0 || 10 === 10 caused unlimited state to immediately revert (#61)

When the Unlimited checkbox fired _emitMaxCycles(0), s.maxCycles became 0.
The next _renderConfig() re-render evaluated `s.maxCycles || 10` as `10`
(because 0 is falsy in JS), so isUnlimited was always false and the
checkbox was immediately unchecked on re-render.

Fix: replace `s.maxCycles || 10` with `s.maxCycles != null ? s.maxCycles : 10`
so that 0 (unlimited) is preserved as a distinct valid value.

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add Career Trailer player and STAR Whiteboard components

Two major new features for the hackathon demo:

1. Career Trailer - cinematic full-screen overlay that auto-plays
   storyboard chapters with TTS voiceover narration. Features
   crossfade transitions, Ken Burns effect on images, progress bar,
   keyboard controls (Space/arrows/Esc), and end card with throughline.
   Handles missing TTS gracefully with timer-based fallback.

2. STAR Whiteboard - real-time visual board showing Situation/Task/
   Action/Result elements extracted during coaching. Hand-drawn SVG
   borders with stroke-dashoffset animation, typewriter text reveal,
   coach color attribution, and "lock in" confirmation. Elements
   appear with entrance animations as coaches extract them.

Also includes:
- Backend STAR extraction endpoint (/api/coaching/extract-star)
- Backend storyboard generation endpoint (/api/coaching/storyboard)
- Gemini native TTS and interleaved image generation support
- Mock data test page (/test) for zero-API-call visual testing
- Zustand store extensions for STAR elements and storyboard state
- Main page layout with three-panel view (whiteboard | chat | sheet)

commit.message:
Re-crop winner portraits, add crop tool, update hero + button styles (#8)

* Re-crop all winner portraits, add crop tool, update hero and button styles

Built a Croppie-based crop tool (/crop-tool.html) with a dev-only API
route that extracts the user's circle crop from originals and regenerates
portrait + thumb via sharp. WYSIWYG: the square Croppie viewport maps
1:1 to the timeline circle with no intermediate reshaping.

Timeline circles now use the square thumb variant instead of the 4:5
portrait, and the circle border is rendered as a box-shadow (outside)
instead of a CSS border (inside) so it doesn't clip the image.

Hero: overlay reduced to 20%, CTA buttons restyled as frosted glass
(Past Champions in translucent gold, Nominate in dark glass), all caps
with tracking and right arrows. Donate and Submit Nomination buttons
get a 3D brass gradient with press travel on hover/active.

Elian Oliva's timeline portrait swapped to the jersey photo while
keeping the ceremony shot for homepage and modal.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix PR review comments: path traversal guards, consistent thumb sizes, crop tool access

- Add path traversal validation to crop API (regex + resolve check)
- Wrap req.json() in try/catch for malformed JSON handling
- Wire up SIZES constant instead of hardcoded resize dimensions
- Unify thumb size to 500x500 across API route, image-utils, and apply-crops
- Add pad-before-extract logic to apply-crops.js (matches API route)
- Align DEFAULT_FOCAL_POINT to 50% 20% (matches manifest normalization)
- Update timeline Image sizes prop to match actual avatar dimensions
- Move crop-tool.html from public/ to tools/, serve via dev-only app route
- Fix crop tool previews to use -thumb instead of -portrait

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Simplify crop route and image manifest

- Loop over SIZES entries instead of 4 copy-pasted sharp calls
- Parallelize jpg+webp writes per size with Promise.all + sharp.clone()
- Hoist filename regex to module-level constant
- Fold quality into SIZES constant (was hardcoded inline)
- Strip redundant focalPoint from all 20 manifest entries (all identical
  to DEFAULT_FOCAL_POINT fallback in WinnerCard)

- Separate JSON parsing from validation (malformed JSON returns 400, not 500)
- Add typeof guards for slug/orig, isFinite check for crop points

  missing file or unreadable image instead of 500)

If settings.json exists but doesn't have statusline config, use jq
to merge it in without overwriting other settings. Creates the file
if it doesn't exist at all.

feat: Discord reset endpoint + onboarding welcome on /link

- POST /api/discord/reset: deletes all Zev channels, recreates fresh,
  registers 16 slash commands, sends System welcome embed + nudge for
  unlinked users. Auth: PIPELINE_SECRET.
- /link now sends tailored onboarding message for new users (explains
  Zev, suggests /ask for onboarding or jump-in commands)
- Added deleteChannel() helper to lib/discord.ts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add room zones to agent card

- Rest zone (left), Work zone (center), Bug zone (right)
- Sprite moves to different zone based on agent state
- 10% chance of failure to demonstrate bug zone

commit.message:
feat: add request logging middleware with X-Request-ID (#96)

* fix: format index.ts body-limit template literal

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add request logging middleware with X-Request-ID

Structured JSON log per request: method, path, status, duration_ms,
request_id. Generates X-Request-ID (or uses incoming header) and
returns it in the response. Skips SSE and OPTIONS. Log level derived
from status code (info/warn/error).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
Fix 3 Codex code review issues: MCP vote body, MRR priority recalc, comment parentId validation

1. MCP vote tool now sends userId in POST body (required for API key auth)
2. Customer identify endpoint recalculates post priorityScore when MRR changes
3. Comment creation validates parentId belongs to the same post

commit.message:
core: add geometry module with rect, sorting, banding, and connected-component labeling

Extracts spatial algorithms from legacy PPP into modern C++20:
- Rect struct with intersection, union, containment, inflate/offset
- Combinability heuristic for zone merging decisions
- Axis-sorted rect ordering (4 directions) using std::stable_sort
- Horizontal/vertical banding (grouping by overlap)
- RLE span extraction from 1-bpp bitmaps
- Connected-component labeling via union-find on RLE spans (4/8 connectivity)
- 7 new test functions covering all geometry operations

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
perf: fix DataFrame fragmentation in features.py

Replace column-by-column assignment with pd.concat in all _add_* functions,
eliminating pandas PerformanceWarning and speeding up build_dataset().
